headline: Models nail tech selloff but miss energy collapse despite $100 oil
summary: AI models successfully predicted a broad market decline with SPY and QQQ falling, though disagreed on magnitude. The day's biggest surprise was XLE's massive 40% crash despite oil hitting $100+ and Middle East tensions, catching both Claude and Gemini completely wrong on energy plays.

consensus_picks.note: Three models correctly called SPY's decline citing hot CPI concerns, while Gemini and Grok went contrarian and got burned.
Both models correctly bet on NVDA's continued AI momentum while Perplexity wrongly called for profit-taking.
Grok expected continuation of the 6% rally while Perplexity correctly called mean reversion.
GPT-4o saw crypto as inflation hedge while Perplexity correctly predicted pressure from rising rates.

best_call.summary: Perplexity perfectly timed QQQ's mean reversion after a 6% rally, nailing both direction and magnitude.

worst_call.summary: Claude's high-conviction energy bet backfired spectacularly as XLE crashed 40% despite $100 oil and Middle East tensions.
